findings.title: North Star Metric: User Engagement
findings.description: The North Star metric for SmartNotify is the Daily Active Users (DAU), which reflects the number of unique users engaging with the notification system daily. This metric is crucial as it indicates overall user engagement and satisfaction with the product.
assumptions: User engagement is directly correlated with the effectiveness of notification prioritization.
recommendations: Monitor DAU trends weekly to assess engagement improvements.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Notification Click-Through Rate (CTR)
findings.description: The Notification Click-Through Rate (CTR) measures the percentage of notifications that users interact with. This is a primary KPI that indicates the effectiveness of notifications in capturing user attention.
assumptions: Higher CTR indicates better notification relevance and user engagement.
recommendations: Aim to increase CTR by optimizing notification content and timing.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Unsubscribe Rate
findings.description: The Unsubscribe Rate measures the percentage of users opting out of notifications. This is a critical input metric that indicates user dissatisfaction and potential notification fatigue.
assumptions: Lower unsubscribe rates correlate with improved user satisfaction.
recommendations: Implement user feedback mechanisms to understand reasons for unsubscribing.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: Average Notifications per User
findings.description: The Average Notifications per User metric tracks the average number of notifications sent to each user. This guardrail metric helps ensure that users are not overwhelmed by excessive notifications.
assumptions: Maintaining a reasonable number of notifications prevents user fatigue.
recommendations: Monitor this metric closely and adjust notification strategies accordingly.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: 95th Percentile Delivery Latency
findings.description: The 95th Percentile Delivery Latency metric measures the time taken to deliver notifications to users. This guardrail metric ensures that latency does not exceed acceptable thresholds, impacting user experience.
assumptions: Lower latency improves user satisfaction and engagement.
recommendations: Implement proposed fixes to reduce latency below 200ms.

findings.title: Measurement Gap: User Preferences for Notification Timing
findings.description: There is currently insufficient data on user preferences for notification timing and grouping. This gap limits the ability to optimize notification delivery effectively.
assumptions: Understanding user preferences is essential for effective notification batching.

evidence.excerpt: The ML model should learn per-user preferences for delivery timing and grouping thresholds.

recommendations: Conduct user surveys or A/B tests to gather data on notification timing preferences.

findings.title: Experiment Opportunity: Notification Content Optimization
findings.description: Conduct experiments to test different notification content strategies to improve CTR and reduce unsubscribe rates. This should include A/B testing of messaging and timing.
assumptions: Different content strategies will yield varying engagement results.
recommendations: Run A/B tests with at least 95% confidence level and 80% power.

summary: The analysis identifies the North Star metric as Daily Active Users, with supporting KPIs including Notification CTR and Unsubscribe Rate. Guardrail metrics are Average Notifications per User and 95th Percentile Delivery Latency. A measurement gap exists regarding user preferences for notification timing, and an experiment opportunity is recommended to optimize notification content.
